LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM AUG-25-2011 TO LAW AND MOTION AT AUG-26-2011, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 - PER MOVING PARTY REQUEST VIA LETTER DTD. 07-28-11. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. WHILE PLAINTIFF ARGUES THERE IS ONLY ONE BREACH OF CONTRACT, THE COMPLAINT INVOLVES MULTIPLE CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE MULTIPLE CONTRACTS ARE AT ISSUE. THE PLAIN READING OF THE '07 CONTRACT SHOWS THAT THERE IS A LOCATION REQUIREMENT. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF MUST AMEND TO REMOVE REFERENCE TO THE '07 CONTRACT. FURTHER, THE COURT OVERRULED THE DEMURRER AS TO THE 2008-2011 POLICIES. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: TAMARA WILLAT-HARRISON, CSR #4609
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-26-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Michael Von Loewenfeldt of Kerr & Wagstaffe LLP (357.8909) for plaintiff and responding party; Amy B. Briggs and Amanda M. Knudsen of Manatt Phelps & Phillips, LLP (291.7451) for defendant and moving party;
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. While plaintiff argues there is only one breach of contract, the complaint involves multiple causes of action because multiple contracts are at issue. The plain reading of the '07 contract shows that there is a location requirement. Accordingly, plaintiff must amend to remove reference to the '07 contract. Further, the court overruled the demurrer as to the 2008-2011 policies. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison, CSR #4609

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2011 Continued To Aug-26-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
